'''YIN-762''' was a [[B1-series battle droid/Legends|B1 battle droid]] who served as a [[Commanding officer/Legends|commander]] in the [[Trade Federation Droid Army/Legends|Droid Army]] of the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]]. In [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]], YIN-762 was in command of a [[YIN-762's base|base]] outside the [[City/Legends|city]] [[Spinnaker/Legends|Spinnaker]] on [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]] during the Trade Federation's [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|invasion]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]].<ref name="GB" />

During the [[invasion of Spinnaker]], the [[OOM command battle droid/Legends|command battle droid]] [[OOM-9/Legends|OOM-9]] sent [[Neimoidian trader]]s, a type of [[Repulsorcraft/Legends|repulsorcraft]], from his [[Unidentified Naboo village (OOM-9)|base]]<ref name="Prima">[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|''Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds'': Prima's Official Strategy Guide]]</ref> to the [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]] in YIN-762's base<ref name="GB" /> to trade for [[Nova crystal/Legends|nova crystals]], which could be used as [[Currency/Legends|currency]]. By trading with the base and destroying Spinnaker's spaceports, OOM-9 was able to achieve [[Viceroy of the Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation Viceroy]] [[Nute Gunray/Legends|Nute Gunray's]] objective of securing 10,000 nova crystals.<ref name="Prima" />

Like all B1-Series [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droids]], YIN-762 was 1.91 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] tall<ref name="Databank" /> and had [[Gender/Legends|masculine]] [[Programmer/Legends|programming]].<ref name="Clone Wars" /> It wielded an [[E-5 blaster rifle/Legends|E-5 blaster rifle]]<ref name="Equipment" /> and had a [[Backpack/Legends|backpack]] equipped with an [[Antenna/Legends|antenna]]. The [[Droid/Legends|droid]] had [[Color/Legends|light tan]] plating with brown markings.<ref name="GB" />

==Behind the scenes==
YIN-762 first appeared in the [[2001]] [[LucasArts]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]''. Its name is given as the player's ally in "Spinnaker's Spoils," the third level of the Trade Federation campaign, although it does not physically appear in the level. YIN-762 is also a unique unit that the player can utilize in the game's scenario editior. The droid's profile image and model are identical to that of other battle droid commanders in the game. In scenarios, YIN-762's model has differences from its profile image, with the tan color seen on other B1-Series battle droids present on its head and parts of its [[Limb/Legends|limbs]], while the color of the player's alignment is featured on its chest and legs.<ref name="GB" />
